# UX Requirements Quality Checklist: Footer Action Consolidation

**Purpose**: Validate the clarity, completeness, consistency, and coverage of the footer-action consolidation specification before implementation planning continues.
**Created**: 2026-06-08
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the /speckit.checklist command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all interactive viewer controls that must move to the footer explicitly enumerated, including the activity-toggle exception? [Completeness, Spec §Clarifications / Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK002 Is the no-duplicate-controls requirement stated for both the navigation bar and the footer, not only one direction? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK003 Are the user stories complete enough to cover the normal action-bearing state, the narrow-layout state, and the no-duplicate rule? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1–3]
- [ ] CHK004 Are the assumptions around unchanged labels, icons, and behavior documented clearly enough to avoid scope creep? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions / Spec §FR-004]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is interactive viewer control defined clearly enough to distinguish action buttons, toggles, and other chrome elements? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities / Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK006 Is footer bar unambiguous enough to distinguish the action footer from the navigation bar chrome? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK007 Is duplicate defined clearly enough to make FR-003 and SC-002 testable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003 / Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK008 Is readable and usable in narrow panels quantified or bounded well enough for consistent interpretation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007 / Spec §SC-003]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Are the user stories consistent with the requirement that every interactive control except the activity toggle moves to the footer? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1 / Spec §FR-001–FR-002]
- [ ] CHK010 Do the success criteria align with the clarified scope and avoid implying any nav-bar action exceptions beyond the activity toggle? [Consistency, Spec §SC-001–SC-004 / Spec §Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK011 Do the assumptions about unchanged labels, icons, and behavior match FR-004 without introducing a hidden exception? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions / Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK012 Do FR-006 and FR-007 stay consistent with the broader claim that the footer is the canonical action surface? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006–FR-007 / Spec §FR-001]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 Can SC-001 be objectively verified without interpreting ambiguous wording about what counts as an action button? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK014 Can SC-002 be measured as a binary no-duplication outcome across every supported viewer state? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK015 Does SC-003 define what qualifies as overlap or blocked activation in a way that reviewers can apply consistently? [Clarity, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK016 Does SC-004 describe an outcome that is specific enough to evaluate without needing additional benchmark context? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are primary, alternate, and narrower-layout scenarios covered across the three user stories? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1–3]
- [ ] CHK018 Are scenarios covered for states that expose only the activity toggle and no other interactive control? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases / Spec §FR-001–FR-002]
- [ ] CHK019 Are scenarios covered for future viewer states that introduce additional interactive controls after this feature ships? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases / Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK020 Are scenarios covered for viewer states where multiple actions appear together in the footer and the nav bar still stays activity-only? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases / Spec §FR-003]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are narrow-panel wrapping and compression cases defined well enough to prevent overlap or hidden controls? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases / Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK022 Are edge cases defined for the single-action and multiple-action states so the activity toggle exception remains consistent? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK023 Are accessibility requirements specified for the consolidated footer controls, including keyboard navigation and screen-reader behavior? [Gap, Spec §FR-007 / Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK024 Are performance or re-render constraints documented, or is the feature intentionally limited to presentation-only changes? [Assumption, Plan §Technical Context / Spec §Assumptions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK025 Is the assumption that the viewer already has a footer area and an activity toggle validated by the current UI architecture? [Assumption, Plan §Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK026 Are the dependencies on the existing footer action catalog and nav-state derivation called out clearly enough to constrain implementation scope? [Dependencies, Plan §Research / Plan §Phase 1]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK027 Is there any conflict between all buttons and actions and the explicit activity-toggle exception that needs stronger precedence wording? [Conflict, Spec §Clarifications / Spec §FR-001–FR-002]
- [ ] CHK028 Does all buttons and actions include non-button interactive affordances such as toggles or menus, and is that scope consistent throughout the spec? [Ambiguity, Spec §Clarifications / Spec §FR-001]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: [x]
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
